Transcrição Completa: How to Double Your Closing Rate in Seven Days
[00:00] It doesn't take years to get good at sales. You can actually double your closing rate in the next seven days. Now, let me show you how cuz it's going to be depending on this one thing I'm about to show you. It's dependent on understanding the stages of the sale. We call this the five stages of the sale. Okay?
[00:20] So, understanding the five stages, why is that so critical for you? Because if you don't understand where you're taking the prospect, you're pretty much winging it. And when you wing anything, how good are you going to be? Not very good. Especially in selling. Okay.
[00:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Stage 1 - Connection Stage
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Primeira etapa do processo de vendas destacada, layout de fluxo sequencial
[00:38] So, the first stage of the sales conversation, now this could be if you sell to consumers or it could be if you sell to businesses. It doesn't really matter. The first stage, we call this the connection stage. Okay? So, this is the connection stage. All right?
[00:52] Now in the connection stage, we want to ask what are called obviously connection questions just like it sounds. Okay. Now there are three reasons why you want to master connection questions and asking the right ones for your industry. Okay.
[01:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: 3 Reasons for Connection Questions
NÚMEROS: 3 razões principais
VISUAL: Lista numerada com três pontos principais, layout vertical
[01:08] Number one, these questions take the focus off you. Just going to abbreviate that and put it on the prospect. Okay? Now, why is that important for you? Most sales people talk too much. When you talk too much, the prospect's guard goes where? It goes up because prospects are used to what? Sales people that talk all the time about their thing.
[01:30] I want to take that focus immediately off you, the salesperson, and you want to take that off you and immediately put on them.
[01:38] Number two is I want to disarm. How do I disarm the prospect to get their guard down? Now, why is it critical for you to master how to get the prospect's guard down? I think you already know this because how many times do you start a sales conversation? Let's say if you cold call and in the first 10 or 15 seconds they say, "Oh, not interested." or we already have somebody for that.
[02:00] That's because we haven't learned how to get their guard down. And I'll show you how to do that in a second.
[02:05] Now, let's say that you're in a conversation and it's going well and 3 to four minutes in the prospect abruptly stops you after you ask a question and they're like, "Hey, enough with the questions. Just tell me how much this is going to cost and I'll tell you if I'm interested." And then you're on the spot. That's because we haven't learned how to get their guard down.
[02:25] There's something we did there that triggered sales resistance. Okay? We don't want sales resistance. Sales resistance is your enemy if you want to really do well in sales. That is enemy number one. How do we get rid of sales resistance from the prospect? Because that causes them not to buy. Okay.
[02:42] And then the third thing that we're doing in the connection phase here is we are taking the prospect from price or cost-based thinking to where where do you want them to be? Results-based thinking.
[02:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Price Thinking → Results Thinking
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Setas de transição, comparação visual entre dois estados mentais
[02:58] Now, what's one of the first things that comes to your mind when a salesperson first starts talking to you? You start thinking what how much is this going to cost? Right? Everybody does. How do I take them from price or cost-based thinking to results based thinking?
[03:12] Now, if I understand the psychology behind what I'm doing here in this part of the conversation, so you can do this stage right here like in as little as like 2 minutes. Like it's pretty easy.
[03:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Connection Stage Results
NÚMEROS: 2 minutos
VISUAL: Timeline mostrando duração curta do estágio, ícones de resultado
[03:25] You can take the focus off you instantly, put them on the prospect. You can disarm the prospect to let their guard down by mastering tonality. Okay? In other trainings, I'll show you that. And you can also reframe them with your first question from price or cost-based thinking to results based thinking.
[03:42] So this stage is important because now why is this so important? Because if you don't master how to disarm the prospect and their guard stays up, what can happen the rest of the sale? Well, you already know, right?
[03:55] You ask questions and you notice a lot of your prospects, unless they're lay down, give you vague, generalized surface level answers. Sometimes one-word answers, right? That's because we haven't learned yet how to disarm them to get them to let their guard down. Okay?
[04:10] So this is the first stage of the sales process. If I don't understand the first stage, all the other stages hard to get. Are you are you starting to get this?
[04:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Stage 2 - Engagement Stage
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Segunda etapa destacada no fluxo de vendas
[04:20] Now, let's go to the second stage. The second stage, this is called the engagement stage. Okay. In the engagement stage, the first part of that stage is we have to ask what are called situation questions. Write this down. This is really important for you right now.
[04:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Situation Questions - 2 Reasons
NÚMEROS: 2 razões principais
VISUAL: Diagrama dividido mostrando duas perspectivas: vendedor e prospect
[04:42] Now, there's two reasons why we have to ask situation questions. The first reason is we have to understand. So you, the salesperson, have to understand the prospect's real situation.
[04:53] Now, why is that so important to you? Because most sales people never find this out. Most sales people ask a few questions and the prospect gives them some vague answers and the salesperson never really understands fully what the real situation is. Okay?
[05:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Iceberg Analogy
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Imagem de iceberg mostrando ponta visível acima da água e massa maior submersa
[05:10] Do we all realize this is kind of like the the tip of the iceberg, like the Titanic? If you just take what the prospect tells you and you think that, hey, I understand their whole situation. What they're usually telling you at the very beginning because how much trust or credibility do you have in the first couple minutes? Not very much, right?
[05:28] So, what they're saying at the very beginning is usually just the surface of what's really going on. Now, why is that critical for you to understand? Because if you don't find out the real situation, how are you ever going to build a gap to where they want to be if you don't really know where they're even at? You're guessing. You never want to guess in sales. That's rule number one.
[05:48] The second reason why I'm asking them is I have to get the prospect to understand their real situation. So, not only do you have to understand the real situation, but more importantly than even you is getting the prospect to understand the real situation.
[06:05] Because when you first start talking to a prospect, do they really understand fully their problems, their situation, what's really going on? Unlikely. They might know that they have a problem. They might know a little bit about their situation, but they don't necessarily know how bad it really is.
[06:22] They don't really necessarily understand the consequences of what happens if they don't do anything about it because they don't do what you do. You see these type of situations and problems all the time. They don't. They're just seeing theirs.
[06:35] And what I always say is the biggest problem in life, the problems that you don't know you have. Because if you don't know what your problems are, how do you ever change? How do you ever fix them?
[06:45] So this part of the engagement stage, situation questions, is finding out what's really going on. All right, I'm going to show you the next part of this. This is really important.
[06:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Problem Awareness Questions
NÚMEROS: 3 razões principais
VISUAL: Seção destacada dentro do Engagement Stage, ênfase visual especial
[06:58] So in the second part of that stage, besides situation questions, we're also going to ask what are called, write this down, problem awareness questions. Now, there's three reasons why we have to master this, okay?
[07:12] And you want to pay attention because this part if we don't master this part there's probably more than likely you're you're not going to make very many sales. Okay.
[07:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Building the Gap
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Diagrama mostrando espaço entre situação atual e situação desejada
[07:24] So problem questions this is where we build the gap. Okay. So you might have heard me say this term now. It seems like everybody's borrow this term for me. How do we build the gap from where they are to where they want to be?
[07:38] It doesn't necessarily mean the prospect is just going to open up to you about all their problems if you ask predictable questions like hey can you tell me some of your challenges or can you tell me what keeps you awake at night or what are some problems that you have?
[07:53] Now why do those questions not work that well? Well, you already know because they're predictable and the prospect knows where those type of questions lead, don't they?
[08:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Problem Finding vs Problem Solving
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Comparação lado a lado, ênfase em "Problem Finding" como prioridade
[08:05] So, the first reason why we ask problem awareness questions is we want to help them find problems they didn't know they have. They didn't even know they had. That is a term we call I'll get a different mark here. That's called problem finding. A lot of you heard of like, hey, you got to be a great problem finder. Problem finder. Problem finding.
[08:25] A lot of you have read books that says, hey, you got to be a great problem solver. Totally agree with that. However, if the prospect does not buy, how are you a problem solver? Problem solving happens after they buy.
[08:38] So if I want to sell more, I have to find more problems. I have to be a problem finder. Why do I want to find more than one problem? I'll tell you why. Okay, I'll give you an example of this. Okay.
[08:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Case Study - Precious Metals Company
NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente
VISUAL: Exemplo de caso real, localização Southern California mencionada
[08:53] So there's a big uh precious metals uh company that we train out in Southern California. And a lot of their salespeople have this problem. The prospects would like answer some ad on TV and they would call in and they would get transferred to a salesperson and they would say, "Hey, what caused you to call in today?"
[09:10] Blah blah blah. They're like, "Well, I'm looking at, you know, uh, protecting myself against inflation by investing in gold and silver." And they're like, "Great. You came to the right place. Let me show you how we do that."
[09:22] And then as they were filling out the paperwork or whatever and supposed to get it done overnight to send in the next day, they noticed a ton of their people that said they were in, they're going to buy, just never bought.
[09:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: One Problem = Risky Strategy
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Diagrama mostrando vulnerabilidade de depender de um único problema
[09:38] The problem is with just finding one problem, and it doesn't matter the industry, is let's say if you sold precious metals for this example, and you're worried about inflation, and then that night you turn on the news. Let's say you're on Fox News or something, okay, with the current administration and they talk about how inflation's going down, how things are getting better, all that stuff.
[10:00] Suddenly, the prospect starts to think, "Oh, maybe I don't need to buy gold and silver because inflation's going down. I'm going to cancel the order." And you lost the deal.
[10:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Multiple Problems = Sale Protection
NÚMEROS: Múltiplas razões mencionadas (diversificação, riqueza, proteção)
VISUAL: Comparação visual: 1 problema vs múltiplos problemas, estratégia de backup
[10:12] However, what if I helped them find out that not only were there other re not only did they want to protect against inflation, but they also wanted to diversify their portfolio instead of just in the stock market, they needed to put some of it into gold and silver.
[10:28] What if I asked more questions to them and help them find out that, you know, hey, maybe I need to diversify in gold as well because a lot of wealthy people are doing that right now.
[10:38] So even if they saw that news saying that said inflation goes down or is going down, right? They're like, "Well, maybe I don't need it for that, but I need it for this reason, this reason, and this reason." And they still buy.
[10:50] That is what problem finding and why problem finding is so important if you want to get in the top 1% in your industry. Okay.
[10:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Root Cause Analysis
NÚMEROS: Top 1% mencionado
VISUAL: Diagrama de causa raiz, árvore de problemas
[11:02] I also need to help them find out, this is really important, the root cause. Most sales people have never been trained how to do root cause of the problems. What is causing the problems?
[11:15] Now, why is that critical for you to help them understand that? Now, you can't just tell them that cuz if you tell them like this is the reason why it's happening, that you're biased, right? They know you're trying to sell something that's going to go in one ear out the other.
[11:28] My questions allow them to tell themselves that. So, if I help them find out the root cause of what's causing the problem, then they start to internalize it. They start to think like, "Oh, I understand why the problem's happening. I need to do something." and they start to emotionally attach to wanting to change said problem or problems. That's why that's that critical.
[11:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Emotional Connection to Problems
NÚMEROS: $50,000 (exemplo de bolsa Birkin), $20 (comparação Target)
VISUAL: Comparação de produtos, análise de necessidades emocionais vs lógicas
[11:53] And then the third thing, which we find that most salespeople never learn how to do, okay? Because they're just not trained how to do this, is we have to help them feel what the problems or it could be, spelling this wrong, problems, could be emotional needs.
[12:10] Let's say if you sell like a $50,000 Birkin purse, doesn't necessarily solve a problem because if you want to just haul around things in a purse, you could go buy a $20 one at Target, right?
[12:22] But it solves an emotional need, higher status. Maybe they want to look a certain way to friends and family, right? They have emotional needs. That's an example of that.
[12:32] So, this part is important here because it helps them feel what the problems are doing to them personally. Okay?
[12:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Fortune 500 CEOs Are Human Too
NÚMEROS: Fortune 500, Fortune 1000
VISUAL: Diagrama cerebral mostrando lado lógico e emocional
[12:42] Even if you sell to Fortune 500 CEOs, which my second career I primarily sold to Fortune 1000 executives, Fortune 500 CEOs. And are CEOs human? Yes. They have a logical side of the brain and emotional side of the brain.
[12:58] So, if we're having a hard time getting them to open up and to feel how the problems are consequences, what those consequences are doing to them personally, that just means we haven't learned how to get them to open up and want to tell us what the problems are doing to them personally.
[13:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: DOUBLE YOUR SALES IN ONE WEEK
NÚMEROS: 2x (dobrar vendas), 1 semana

[13:30] So instead of a predictable question like can you tell me some problems that you're having or some challenges you're having. Let's say if I I don't know I'm just going to make I'm going to make up a generic one here.
[13:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Example Questions
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Exemplos de perguntas reformuladas, comparação antes/depois
[13:45] So help me understand you're already with XYZ company. What's what's caused you to feel like you might want to look at someone else? See, that's kind of generic. I could use that for a lot of different issues.
[13:58] I wouldn't use that if I'm cold calling because I'm the one that reached out. I wouldn't use that for door to door because I'm the one who reached out. But if it's an inbound or outbound lead, I would language that first problem or just question that way.
[14:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Life Insurance Example
NÚMEROS: $50,000 (apólice de trabalho)
VISUAL: Exemplo específico de indústria, aplicação prática
[14:15] Or let's say if I sold life insurance. Okay. Well, help me understand. You've already got this $50,000 work policy. What's what's caused you to feel like that might not be enough for sending the kids when you when you pass?
[14:28] Well, it's good, but it's not going to cover this. and they start telling you but more importantly telling who themselves why they want to change. Okay, this part is important.
[14:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Solution Awareness Questions
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Terceira subcategoria do Engagement Stage
[14:40] Now let's go to this third part here. I'm going to show you this. These are called solution awareness questions. A mistake a lot of sales people make is maybe they help them find one or two problem and then they try to close them and then they notice the prospect still doesn't buy.
[14:58] Why does that happen to you? Because you it's more like 50% of the sale is helping them find problems.
[15:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: TWO BIGGEST EMOTIONAL DRIVERS
NÚMEROS: 50% da venda, 2 drivers principais
VISUAL: Destaque dramático, hierarquia de motivadores emocionais
[15:08] I want to show you something that I almost forgot. The two biggest emotional drivers that causes a human being to want to change, that means buy from you, are these pain of their current situation or their past history and then fear of future pain. Okay.
[15:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Pain + Fear > Pleasure
NÚMEROS: Pleasure = terceiro lugar distante
VISUAL: Ranking de motivadores, pain e fear no topo, pleasure em terceiro
[15:28] Pain and the fear of future pain drives human beings to change way faster than pleasure. And I'll tell you why. A lot of people have this mixed up. I see it in marketing and sales, okay?
[15:40] Because they say, "Oh, it's pain drives change and then moving towards what you want, pleasure." Pleasure is a distant third.
[15:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Fitness Example - Fear vs Pleasure
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Exemplo de praia/biquíni, análise psicológica de motivação
[15:50] Let's say if you sell fitness or weight loss or anything like that and the prospect says, "Well, yeah, I I want to get in shape because I want to look good this summer on the beach. I want to look good in my bikini on the beach."
[16:05] Well, you would say, "Well, the drive towards pleasure." I would say no. What's their fear? Their fear is they're not going to look very good on the beach. They're just not telling you that. That's their fear.
[16:18] For them to say, "I want to look good in bikini." Means they have a fear of what? Being judged in the bikini. Not looking good in the bikini. That's their fear of future pain. Okay, you're starting to get this now. You're smart. I like this. Okay.
[16:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Future Pacing
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Conceito de visualização futura, linha do tempo
[16:35] So, with solution awareness questions, okay, the main reason why I want to ask them is because this is where I've got this is called future pacing. Some of you might have heard of this future pacing.
[16:48] So, not only do I have to help them find problems they didn't realize they had, I got to help them feel that pain and have that fear that that pain could get worse or even if they don't have pain now, that there could be pain in the future if they don't do something now. Fear future pain.
[17:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: See AND Feel the Future
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Ênfase em experiência sensorial completa, visualização emocional
[17:08] I've got to get them to see and feel. Notice I said feel. It's very important here. What their future looks like once the problems or emotional needs are solved. Okay, that's called future pacing.
[17:22] I have to get them to see and feel, visually see and feel what the future looks like once the newfound problems or emotional needs are solved.
[17:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Industry Examples
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Múltiplos exemplos: terapia matrimonial, CRM, várias indústrias
[17:32] Let's say if I'm a psychologist and I'm selling marriage therapy or something like that, I've got to get them to see and feel what the future looks like with their spouse once they solve all these uh, you know, lack of intimacy problems or, you know, lack of connection problems or whatever.
[17:50] If I'm, let's say, if I'm selling like CRM, I've got to get them to see and feel what the future looks like when they're more organized now because they have the right CRM and their sales people are making more sales. I got to get them to see and feel that. Okay?
[18:05] So, whatever you sell, you got to get them to see and feel that visually. We want to stay away from surface level words like, you know, what are you looking for in a solution? That's just surface level. Okay? So many more things I could show you there. Okay?
[18:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Consequence Questions
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Quarta subcategoria do Engagement Stage, ênfase em urgência
[18:22] We're going to move on to the third part of stage two. Let's go over here. We have to get them to focus on the consequences. These are called consequence questions. Okay?
[18:32] So, we have to get them to focus on the consequences of what happens if they don't do anything. If they don't buy, if they if the problem doesn't get solved. Okay?
[18:42] So, there's a couple reasons why I want to ask these questions. And you want to write these down. This is like vital for you. Okay?
[18:50] Because how many of you feel that the sales conversation or conversations went well, but they just don't feel any urgency to buy? How many of you felt that?
[19:00] There's a reason that is because you haven't mastered how to ask what are called consequence questions. You haven't mastered this stage of the sale yet. Okay?
[19:10] And consequence questions sometimes can just be one consequence question. First of all, I need to get them to feel what the consequences are. Spelling this wrong. Consequences are if nothing changes.
[19:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Example Consequence Questions
NÚMEROS: $5 milhões por mês (exemplo de meta), 3 meses (timeline)
VISUAL: Exemplos de perguntas específicas, aplicações B2B
[19:28] Like if you're selling anything to a business to help them grow, you have to get them to see what the and feel what the consequence are if if they don't grow. So what happens if if this stays the same and you guys are never able to scale to $5 million a month? Like what's going to happen at that point? Consequence question. Okay.
[19:48] Have you thought about what would happen if this actually gets worse? That's a generic consequence question. Okay.
[19:55] What happens if you don't do anything about this? If you keep getting these leads that your salespeople can't get a hold of and now your boss knows that your numbers are down the next 3 months, like what could happen to your job at that point? Consequence questions.
[20:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Timing is Critical
NÚMEROS: 3/4 do caminho na conversa
VISUAL: Timeline da conversa, posicionamento estratégico das perguntas
[20:12] Now, that's not something you're going to ask at the beginning of a conversation because you don't have much trust and credibility. This is like three-fourths of the way in after you've built that gap.
[20:25] So, after you've got them to see what the future looks like once the new found problems are solved, I'm going to rip that away and get them to defend themselves on why they need to change.
[20:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Self-Defense = Urgency
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Conceito de auto-defesa emocional, construção de urgência
[20:40] Now, if you could master getting the prospect to defend themselves on why they have to change, would you say that would build a lot of urgency? Yes. Because they're basically telling you, defending themselves, why they have to do this, why they have to change? Now, that builds massive urgency.
[20:58] Now, I have to use the right tonality. I have to know what questions to ask for the industry for that. But I'm showing you this, okay? This is really important as far as overview.
[21:08] And then I'm also going to use them to question their way of thinking on why they haven't done anything about changing. Now, you don't do this defensively. So, what's what's prevented you from doing this in the past, though? Okay.
[21:25] So, I'm finding out they're going internal like what's held them back from solving this problem in the past. Okay. On why they haven't done anything. So, it's all how you ask that.
[21:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Most Salespeople Never Master This
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Destaque de diferenciação competitiva, oportunidade de vantagem
[21:38] This right here is a very important stage if you want to build urgency in the sale. Most salespeople have never mastered this stage of the sale. Okay? Never. I want you to master that because it's going to help you sell a lot more.
[21:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Stage 3 - Transition Stage
NÚMEROS: Estágio mais curto dos 5
VISUAL: Terceiro estágio destacado, ênfase em brevidade
[21:55] Now, let's go to the next stage. This is important here. All right. Now, we're going to get into stage three. Okay? So stage two is the longest stage. That's the engagement stage.
[22:08] You're asking situation questions, problem awareness to build the gap, situations to find out the real situation. Remember, uh problem awareness questions to build the gap, solution awareness questions, get them to see what the future feels like once problems are solved, and then consequence questions to get them defend themselves on why they feel like they have to change now.
[22:30] So stage three. Okay, this stage we call the transition stage. Okay, so the transition stage. All right. Now, this stage is pretty quick actually out of the five stages, the quickest one.
[22:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: B2C One-Call Close
NÚMEROS: 1 conversa para fechar
VISUAL: Fluxo B2C, transição direta para apresentação
[22:45] So, there's one or two things you have to understand. If you sell to consumers and you're in a one conversation close, meaning that uh maybe you sell door-to-door, you sell whatever it is, life insurance, and it's a one call close or one conversation close, whatever, you're then going to transition easily into your presentation. Okay?
[23:05] You're just transitioning. It's just basically whatever the next step is in your sales process. Okay?
[23:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: High-Ticket B2C Example
NÚMEROS: $500,000 (piscinas de luxo)
VISUAL: Exemplo de venda complexa B2C, múltiplos passos
[23:15] Now, that doesn't mean if you sell to consumers that you could, let's say, if you sold like luxury pools that are $500,000, it's probably not going to be a one conversation close.
[23:25] The next step, you might transition into um you know, going over the proposal is the second step. Okay. The third step could be going into the design room with the company to pick out the design. So, you're just transitioning into the next step of the process of the sales process. Okay?
[23:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: B2B Sales Cycles
NÚMEROS: 9-12 meses (ciclos enterprise)
VISUAL: Comparação de diferentes ciclos de venda B2B
[23:45] Now, if you sell to companies and this I'm not giving you straight jacket interpretation. There are some companies like if you sold B2B, there are some companies if you sell like to a very very small company that it might be a one conversation close. Okay?
[24:00] or if you sell at the enterprise level and let's say you have a a 9 or 12 month sales cycle. Okay, I'm just transitioning into let's say if I sold SaaS that would be transition into booking the next step demo.
[24:15] So after I have that discovery conversation as some would call it, I then have to transition into whatever the next step is.
[24:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: DEATH OF SALESPEOPLE
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Alerta dramático, aviso crítico sobre agendamento
[24:25] Most sales people miss this part here. Most sales people are like, "Okay, uh well does that sound good?" Uh okay. and the prospect's like, "Well, I'll get I'll get back to you next week." "Okay, well, let's talk next week about it." And then magically what? The prospect obviously is not going to call you back there.
[24:45] Unless there is a next step, unless you're transitioning to the next step, unless you're selling in a one conversation close. Like I said, every industry is different.
[24:55] So, if you sell in one conversation, you're just closing it there. If you're in a industry where, like I said, maybe you sell, you know, cyber security to Bank of America or something like that, that's not going to be one conversation close, right?
[25:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Next Step Examples
NÚMEROS: 9 meses (exemplo de ciclo)
VISUAL: Lista de possíveis próximos passos: demo, reunião com decisor, proposta
[25:12] So that might take a nine month sales cycle or whatever. I'm just transitioning to whatever that next step is. Next step demo could be next step meeting with the department head who's another decision maker. It could be a next step you go over the proposal. Okay?
[25:28] That's all going to depend on your industry. But the point is is that you have to have something booked on the calendar. Otherwise, there's really no sale. Okay?
[25:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: CRITICAL RULE: Calendar or Death
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Ênfase máxima, regra absoluta de vendas
[25:40] So, if you're in a in a in a in a type of industry where it's not a one conversation close, you have to like, let me repeat this, it is the death of salespeople, if you do not have a next step booked on your calendar and their calendar, a next step appointment. Otherwise, it's pretty much over.
[26:00] If you ever buy into their story that I'll call you back next week, more than likely, they're not going to call you back. And you already know this. Okay, that is stage three.
[26:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Stage 4 - Presentation
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Quarto estágio, apresentação da solução
[26:12] Now, stage four, I'm going to come over here. Stage four is just really the presentation. Okay, now we don't have time on this on this uh training here to go over like how to structure the presentation. We do that in uh you know in different courses that clients come into.
[26:30] but the stage four is like you're just presenting now. You're going over how what you do helps them solve all these problems you found. And this is where you typically bring up the funding or funds that they need to solve the problem so they can get XYZ result. That is stage four.
[26:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Stage 5 - Commitment Stage (Closing)
NÚMEROS: Estágio 5
VISUAL: Estágio final destacado, ênfase em fechamento
[26:50] And then finally we come to good old stage five. This is a lot of fun. We're going to do more of these for you guys this way. This is stage five. Okay, stage five. Very, very important. This is where you close the deal. Okay, we call this the commitment stage, also known as closing. Okay.
[27:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: The Closing Myth
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Mito vs realidade sobre fechamento de vendas
[27:12] Now, a lot of salespeople have been sold this lie that when, you know, I always get off stage. I'll be at an event. I'll get off stage after uh doing a training and one of the first questions people ask me is like, "Hey, Jeremy, I need to get better at closing. Like, what are some closing questions I can get better so I can close more deals?"
[27:32] And I'm like, "Let me ask you, does the prospect decide to buy?" When you ask a closing question, is that when they decide to buy? I'm Do they decide to buy when you say, "Do you want the red one or the blue one?" The option close or why don't you give it a try? Is that when they decide to buy?
[27:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Decision Happens BEFORE Closing Question
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Timeline mostrando decisão ocorrendo antes da pergunta de fechamento
[27:52] No. If they say, "I want the red one." That means they what? They had already decided to buy before you asked them that question. And they decided to buy because you built enough pain. You built enough gap from where they are to where they want to be that they already made that decision by the time you asked your closing question. Okay?
[28:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Closing = 1-2% of Sale
NÚMEROS: 5% (estimativa antiga), 1-2% (realidade)
VISUAL: Gráfico de pizza mostrando pequena porcentagem do fechamento
[28:18] So, I'm the point is I'm trying to uh I'm going to get with you is that closing like I used to say closing was like 5%. In all reality, it's like 1 to 2% of the sale because the close is in the discovery part of that conversation. Okay?
[28:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Where The REAL Close Happens
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Lista de momentos reais de fechamento ao longo do processo
[28:38] The the close is disarming the prospect where they let their guard down. The close is, you know, learning how to help them find problems they didn't realize they have or they start to feel that pain, that emotional pain.
[28:52] They start to internalize what the pain's doing to them and they start to internalize the consequences of what happens if they don't do anything. It's also when getting them to see and feel what the future looks like once the newfound problems are solved. Like that's when they start closing themselves. That's where the close is.
[29:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Commitment Questions
NÚMEROS: 2-3 perguntas
VISUAL: Exemplos de perguntas de comprometimento simples
[29:12] So basically commitment stage. I'm asking like maybe two to three questions at the end. I mean here's a generic one I can give you like do do you feel like this could be the answer for you? Yeah, I do. or yeah, but let's say they yeah, I do whole time. Why do you feel like it is? That's a probing question.
[29:32] So, the first one's a commitment question. Do you feel like this could be the answer? That's a commitment question.
[29:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: 2 Reasons for Commitment Stage
NÚMEROS: 2 razões
VISUAL: Duas funções principais do estágio de comprometimento
[29:42] So, in commitment stage, there's two reasons why I'm doing that. I'm getting them to commit to take the next step and purchase your solution. Okay. So this is the next logical step is you're committing to take the next step and purchase what you're offering. Okay.
[30:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Micro Commitments
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Escada de pequenos comprometimentos levando ao grande
[30:02] Now the second reason why we ask commitment question these are called micro commitments. Now this is really important. I didn't know if I was going to bring this up. Okay.
[30:12] So micro commitments are smaller steps all these markers. Smaller steps that lead to the larger step of them buying. Okay, such as book like let's say you sold SaaS which is one of the top five industries you're trained is just booking the next step demo that is actually a micro commitment.
[30:32] So every time they have a micro commitment every time they do that next step that little thing that micro commitment leads them to what the ultimate the ultimate commitment of them buying what you're offering. Are you starting to get this?
[30:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Micro Commitment Examples
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Lista de micro comprometimentos: reuniões, emails, detalhes
[30:50] Next step meeting with the department head next step you know going through some details you emailed them before the appointment. Every time they do those things, they are committing. Those are smaller steps, micro commitments that lead to that uh that bigger step of purchasing. Okay.
[31:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: What is NEPQ?
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Introdução da metodologia NEPQ
[31:12] Now, you're probably wondering at this point, Jeremy, how do I use this? Because here's what we did. What I just showed you is what is called NPQ. Okay. Now, you might have heard of this.
[31:25] NPQ is a sales methodology I developed from my background um in university studying behavioral science uh to become a psychologist. Okay.
[31:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: N = Neuro
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Primeiro componente da metodologia, ícone cerebral
[31:38] so neuro stands for the brain. Okay, so neuro stands for the brain. How it works in conjunction with somebody's nervous system. Kind of important if I want to master how to get the guard out. Remember we talked about that in stage one. Okay.
[31:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: E = Emotion
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Segundo componente, ícone de conexão emocional
[31:58] E E stands for emotion. How do I master the emotional connection where that prospect feels like you understand them, their needs, their desires, their wants, even their fears without buying into their story. That's emotional connection. Okay.
[32:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: P = Persuasion (Self-Persuasion)
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Terceiro componente, ênfase em auto-persuasão
[32:18] The P stands for persuasion. How do I get the prospect to actually persuade themselves? That's self-persuasion. According to the data, the highest form of persuasion is getting somebody to persuade themselves, not you trying to persuade them. Okay? Very low in the totem pole.
[32:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Q = Questioning
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Quarto componente, técnicas de questionamento
[32:40] And then Q stands for questioning. So, how do you learn how to reframe the questions you're asking now to interrupt the prospect's patterns, okay? To get them to open up emotionally. That is NEPQ.
[32:55] Now, at this point, you're probably asking yourself, "How do I take everything that Jeremy just showed me here in this basic YouTube video, and how do I apply it to what I sell?" Okay? How do I sell more using this? And that's smart of you to think. Okay?
[33:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Contact Information
NÚMEROS: 480-637-2944
VISUAL: Número de telefone para contato, call to action
[33:15] So, I'm going to give you our number. Text me right now and just ask me a question. 480-637-2944 Just text us, either myself or one of our sales trainers. We'll respond back to you. Hope that helped.
[33:30] And look, your next step, go ahead and click on the subscribe button so you can learn how to sell more. This is an overview of how to structure your sales conversations. Take it, use it, commit to mastery.
[33:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Commit to Mastery
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Chamada final para ação, conceito de maestria vs amadorismo
[33:48] I can assure you when you commit to mastery and you're not like all those sales people out there that are wingers that keep winging it or dabblers who dabble in this or know-it-allers who think they know everything and you commit to mastery and you actually get a plan, step-by-step plan taught by somebody who's actually mastered it themselves, you're going to go really far in sales and I know that's why you follow me. up. Hope that helped you today.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
Possibilidade de dobrar taxa de fechamento em 7 dias (2x)
5 estágios do processo de vendas
3 razões para perguntas de conexão
2 razões para perguntas situacionais
3 razões para perguntas de consciência de problema
50% da venda = encontrar problemas
2 maiores drivers emocionais (dor + medo)
Prazer = terceiro lugar distante
Fechamento = apenas 1-2% da venda (anteriormente estimado em 5%)
2-3 perguntas de comprometimento no final
$50,000 (exemplo bolsa Birkin)
$20 (comparação Target)
$500,000 (exemplo piscinas de luxo)
$5 milhões/mês (exemplo de meta empresarial)
$50,000 (exemplo apólice de seguro)
9-12 meses (ciclos de venda enterprise)
3-4 minutos (quando resistência tipicamente aparece)
2 minutos (duração do Connection Stage)
3/4 do caminho (quando fazer perguntas de consequência)
Top 1% da indústria (objetivo)
Fortune 500/1000 (experiência do instrutor)
Contato: 480-637-2944
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
METODOLOGIA NEPQ:
N = Neuro (cérebro + sistema nervoso)
E = Emotion (conexão emocional)
P = Persuasion (auto-persuasão)
Q = Questioning (reformulação de perguntas)
OS 5 ESTÁGIOS DA VENDA:
CONNECTION STAGE (Conexão)
Tirar foco do vendedor, colocar no prospect
Desarmar para baixar a guarda
Mudar de pensamento de preço para resultados
Duração: ~2 minutos
Dominar tonalidade
ENGAGEMENT STAGE (Engajamento) - ESTÁGIO MAIS LONGO
a) Situation Questions (Perguntas Situacionais)
Vendedor entender situação real
Prospect entender situação real
Analogia do iceberg (superfície vs realidade completa)
Nunca adivinhar em vendas
b) Problem Awareness Questions (Consciência de Problema)
Problem Finding vs Problem Solving
Encontrar múltiplos problemas (não apenas um)
Descobrir causa raiz
Fazer sentir as consequências pessoalmente
Construir o GAP (onde estão vs onde querem estar)
Evitar perguntas previsíveis
Exemplo: empresa de metais preciosos (inflação + diversificação + riqueza)
c) Solution Awareness Questions (Consciência de Solução)
Future Pacing (visualização do futuro)
Fazer VER e SENTIR o futuro resolvido
Aplicável a qualquer indústria
Evitar perguntas superficiais
d) Consequence Questions (Perguntas de Consequência)
Fazer sentir consequências de não mudar
Fazer defender-se sobre por que precisam mudar
Questionar por que não mudaram antes
Construir urgência massiva
Usar tonalidade correta
Timing: 3/4 do caminho na conversa
TRANSITION STAGE (Transição) - ESTÁGIO MAIS CURTO
B2C one-call close: transição para apresentação
B2B/vendas complexas: agendar próximo passo
CRÍTICO: sempre ter próximo passo no calendário
"Morte do vendedor" = não agendar próximo passo
Nunca aceitar "te ligo semana que vem"
Exemplos: demo, reunião com decisor, proposta, design room
PRESENTATION STAGE (Apresentação)
Apresentar como solução resolve problemas encontrados
Discutir investimento/funding necessário
Conectar com resultados desejados
COMMITMENT STAGE (Comprometimento/Fechamento)
Fechamento = apenas 1-2% da venda real
Verdadeiro fechamento acontece durante discovery
2-3 perguntas de comprometimento
Micro Commitments (pequenos passos)
Exemplos: agendar demo, reunião, revisar emails
Decisão já foi tomada antes da pergunta de fechamento
DRIVERS EMOCIONAIS:
Dor da situação atual/história passada (primário)
Medo de dor futura (primário)
Prazer (distante terceiro)
Exemplo: fitness/praia = medo de julgamento, não prazer
TÉCNICAS ESPECÍFICAS:
Evitar perguntas previsíveis ("quais seus desafios?")
Reformular perguntas para interromper padrões
Fazer prospect se persuadir (auto-persuasão)
Nunca comprar a história do prospect
Fazer prospect defender necessidade de mudança
Construir confiança antes de perguntas profundas
ESTRUTURA:
Framework de 5 estágios sequenciais
Cada estágio depende do anterior
Stage 2 (Engagement) = mais longo e crítico
Stage 3 (Transition) = mais curto
Fluxo lógico: Connection → Engagement → Transition → Presentation → Commitment
Não pular etapas
Não "improvisar" (winging it)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Exemplos de produtos: $50K bolsa Birkin vs $20 Target
Vendas high-ticket: piscinas $500K
Metas empresariais: $5M/mês
Seguros: apólices $50K
Ciclos enterprise: 9-12 meses
Investimento necessário discutido no Stage 4
ERROS COMUNS A EVITAR:
Falar demais (vendedor típico)
Não desarmar o prospect
Encontrar apenas 1 problema
Fazer perguntas previsíveis
Não construir urgência
Aceitar "te ligo depois"
Não agendar próximo passo
Focar em técnicas de fechamento
Ser "winger", "dabbler" ou "know-it-all"
Tentar persuadir ao invés de fazer auto-persuasão
DIFERENCIAÇÃO COMPETITIVA:
Maioria dos vendedores nunca domina consequence questions
Maioria não sabe construir urgência
Maioria não entende os 5 estágios
Maioria improvisa sem estrutura
Oportunidade de duplicar vendas em 1 semana apenas dominando Problem Awareness
DURAÇÃO: ~34 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional/treinamento de vendas com apresentação visual METODOLOGIA: NEPQ (Neuro-Emotional Persuasion Questioning) INSTRUTOR: Jeremy Miner (background em ciências comportamentais/psicologia) APLICAÇÃO: B2B e B2C, todos
